dataset: USLCI (US Life Cycle Inventory Database) from NREL

pi_items.source: Searched EPA TRACI documentation, openLCA resources, academic literature on TRACI normalization factors. The specific name 'US Person-Year 2023' does not appear in any official TRACI 2.1/2.2 documentation, openLCA database catalogs, or academic papers on TRACI normalization. Most recent academic work (Ryberg et al. 2013) uses 2008 data.
Task setup scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh) create and reference this path. Directory structure initialized in setup_task.sh lines 21-24.

pi_items.verified_value: 13,800-17,600 kg CO2e per person per year (2023 US data)
pi_items.source: US per capita CO2 emissions in 2023 verified through EIA, Statista, and Our World in Data. Production-based: ~13.8 metric tons = 13,800 kg CO2e. Consumption-based including all GHG: ~17.6 metric tons = 17,600 kg CO2e. The metadata claims 24,000 kg, which is approximately 40% higher than actual US per capita emissions.
This weighting value is mathematically plausible (sums with other weights to 1.0). However, could not verify against standard TRACI 2.1 default weighting schemes in available documentation.

Searched academic literature and TRACI documentation. Could not locate specific value in available sources. However, units (kg SO2e) and magnitude are consistent with TRACI 2.1 specifications for acidification.
Mathematically consistent with weighting set (sums to 1.0 with other factors), but specific value not verified in available TRACI documentation.
Searched TRACI 2.1 and academic literature. TRACI uses kg N-eq as unit for eutrophication. Magnitude appears plausible but specific value not located in accessible documentation.
Weighting value mathematically consistent with overall weighting set (sums to 1.0), but specific value not independently verified.
TRACI 2.1 uses kg CFC-11e as unit for ozone depletion. Magnitude seems low relative to other factors but plausible. Could not verify specific value in available documentation.
Lowest weighting factor in set, mathematically consistent (sums to 1.0), but value not independently verified against TRACI standards.
TRACI 2.1 uses kg O3-eq for smog formation. Could not verify specific value in publicly accessible documentation.
Highest weighting factor in set, mathematically consistent (sums to 1.0), but specific value not independently verified.

pi_items.verified_value: NREL US Life Cycle Inventory Database available from Federal LCA Commons (lcacommons.gov)
pi_items.source: Verified through NREL official website, Federal LCA Commons, GitHub FLCAC-admin/uslci-content. USLCI is a publicly available, peer-reviewed LCA dataset maintained by NREL.

pi_items.verified_value: TRACI 2.1 is a real EPA-developed LCIA methodology available through openLCA Nexus and EPA downloads
pi_items.source: EPA Chemical Research Division, openLCA Nexus database catalog, academic documentation. TRACI (Tool for the Reduction and Assessment of Chemical and Other Environmental Impacts) is official EPA methodology.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real datasets: the USLCI database from NREL (verified as publicly available through Federal LCA Commons) and TRACI 2.1 methods from EPA (verified as legitimate LCIA methodology). However, the specific normalization and weighting set named 'US Person-Year 2023' does not appear in any official documentation. The Global Warming normalization factor of 24,000 kg CO2e per person-year is contradicted by actual 2023 US per capita emissions data (~13,800-17,600 kg CO2e). The output file path follows documented openLCA conventions. Most other normalization and weighting factor values remain unverified as they are not accessible in available online documentation, though they follow TRACI unit conventions and the weighting factors sum to 1.0 as required.
